title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 12
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear a teacher talking to a student about a history project.
items.audio_script: Man: How is your history project going? I know you have been working on it this week. || Woman: Quite well. I have already finished the introduction, and I thought I needed to add a poster as well. || Man: I checked the instructions this morning. the map is Sam's part, and a poster is not required. [1]What you still need is a short conclusion at the end[/1]. || Woman: That is helpful. I can do it this evening and check everything again before I send it in tomorrow morning.
items.question: What does the student still need to add?
items.explanation: The student still needs to add a short conclusion.

items.context: You hear two friends arranging Saturday afternoon.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are we still going to play tennis on Saturday afternoon? I told my family I would be home later. || Boy: That was our first idea, but the courts are closed for repairs. We could go to watch a film, though there is nothing they want to see. [2]We are going to visit the aquarium instead[/2]. || Girl: That sounds good. Shall we meet near the station? || Boy: Yes, at four. I will book our places now, so there should not be a problem when we arrive.
items.question: What are they going to do?
items.explanation: They decide to visit the aquarium.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a train journey.
items.audio_script: Boy: I need to check the time for the train to Brighton. I wrote down nine fifteen, but I am not sure it is correct. || Girl: It changed this morning because work is being done on the railway line. [3]It starts at nine forty-five now[/3], and we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Boy: I am glad I asked. I would have arrived at the wrong time. || Girl: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go back home first.
items.question: What time does the train leave?
items.explanation: The train now leaves at nine forty-five.

items.context: You hear two friends discussing a concert journey.
items.audio_script: Woman: How are you travelling to the concert hall on Saturday? I thought you were going by car. || Man: I planned to, but parking near the hall is very expensive. I considered going by bus, but the buses will be delayed by roadworks. [4]I am going by underground instead[/4]. || Woman: That sounds sensible. What time will you arrive? || Man: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I checked the timetable online, so we have plenty of time, and I will message you when I leave home.
items.question: How will they get to the concert?

items.context: You hear two people talking about a mobile phone.
items.audio_script: Man: How are you getting on with your mobile phone? I heard there was a problem with it. || Woman: I first thought the screen was cracked, and my brother wondered whether the camera was blurred. But the shop checked it carefully this morning. [5]The speaker does not work at all[/5]. || Man: Can they fix that before your journey on Friday? || Woman: They think so. Now they know what is wrong, it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief, because I need to use it this weekend.
items.question: What is wrong with the phone?
items.explanation: The phone's speaker does not work.

items.context: You hear two friends preparing for a picnic.
items.audio_script: Girl: Have you packed for the picnic tomorrow? I was going to take plastic cups. Is that still right? || Boy: It was on the first list, but Mia has cups already, but nobody has anything to serve the food on. [6]You need to bring paper plates instead[/6]. || Girl: Good thing I asked. I nearly closed my bag without it. || Boy: Put it in now and check again before you leave. The organiser said we must be ready on time tomorrow, because the group cannot wait for anyone who forgets something important.
items.question: What should they bring?
items.explanation: They need to bring paper plates.

items.context: You hear two people choosing a birthday present.
items.audio_script: Boy: Did you buy a scarf for Maya's birthday? I saw one yesterday and thought it would be suitable. || Girl: No. I looked at a scarf, and I nearly chose a necklace, but she already has many scarves and does not often wear jewellery. [7]I bought a photo frame in the end[/7]. || Boy: That sounds like the right choice. It is more useful than the other things. || Girl: I hope so. I kept the receipt in case I need to change it later. She can put it on her desk too.
items.question: What present did the speaker buy?
items.explanation: The speaker bought a photo frame.
